title: Llama7B score32/w16 RTL exact-divide generation quality
hypothesis: The score32/w16 floating-quantized softmax quality gate passes, while the score32/w16 reciprocal-LUT q16 RTL softmax gate fails. Running the same gate with RTL exact division isolates whether the failure is caused primarily by reciprocal approximation or by the shared RTL softmax exponent/weight approximation.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the score32/w16 RTL exact-divide softmax candidate preserve bounded greedy generation and teacher-forced reference-token likelihood on a 7B checkpoint?
include: run native-checkpoint reference and score32/w16 RTL exact-divide candidate on the same bounded prompt set
measure teacher-forced reference-token NLL under reference and candidate
measure free-running greedy token match and first divergence step
classify whether exact division is enough to recover the failed reciprocal-LUT q16 quality gate

expected_benefit: separate reciprocal approximation error from the shared RTL softmax weight/exponent approximation
avoid spending PPA effort on reciprocal variants if exact division also fails generation quality
identify whether the next precision-recovery job should target reciprocal bits or exponent/softmax modeling
risks: bounded greedy generation is not full task accuracy
prompt and generation-step defaults may miss longer-horizon divergence
quality outcome remains checkpoint and distribution dependent

required_evaluations.objective: Run bounded native-checkpoint generation/NLL quality for score32/w16 RTL exact-divide softmax after reciprocal-LUT q16 failed the same gate.

expected_result.reason: If RTL exact-divide passes, recover by improving reciprocal approximation. If it fails, recover by replacing the shared RTL softmax exponent/weight approximation.
